nextSession.thesis: MORNING (7/16, ~11:00 ET), LIVE intraday off the 7/15 close, targeting the rest of today's regular session into 7/17 OPEX — the market is OPEN. Structure first: SPY 751.11 stays bull-stacked (20>50>200), +1.12% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.38% over the 200-day (~693.0), only −0.86% off the 52-week high (~757.6), mom126/252 +8.84%/+22.07% — 5th distinct daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's forming 7/16 intraday. What CHANGED this morning is tactical, not structural: the extended leaders are giving back while laggards/defensives bid. QQQ 706.95 is STILL below its 50-day and slipping further (−1.57% vs −0.54% pre-market 7/15, mom5 −2.26) — the reclaim is moving AWAY, not toward. Momentum names are the weak tape (SPMO mom5 −5.15, VGT −3.04) and semis are rolling hard (SOXX rsMom1m −15.62, MRVL −22.76 / MU −14.19 / QCOM −10.43 on the 5-day, 0/12 up-momentum) though SOXX is still +35.83% over its 200-day. Against that, the LONE structural crack is tentatively repairing — MSFT 404.6 ticked back above its 50-day (~401.7, +0.72%, mom5 +5.27) for the first time (still bear-stacked, −7.72% below its 200-day). RRG: XLE is the cleanest rotation INTO (accel +2.27, rsMom 102.5, one cross from Leading); XLF is back to Leading (rsRatio 101.65, highest on the board); defensives are bidding (XLP Leading accel +1.27, XLRE Leading +1.01, XLU Improving) as XLI (accel −1.58) and XLV (Weakening) roll. VIX ticked up to 16.49 from 15.67 (7/15 AH) — still low/calm but firming as the leaders wobble. Posture: HOLD the bull-stack (do NOT add index beta with QQQ below its 50-day); ROTATE toward XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched, rolling semis into any relief; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT's tentative 50-day reclaim into its 7/29 capex guide; carry a small defined-risk hedge through 7/17 OPEX and the underpriced 7/29 hike tail.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: The core stays structurally bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +8.84%/+22.07%; QQQ mom63 +11.03, mom252 +27.6; SPMO +14.18% / VGT +14.64% over their 200-days). But this morning's tape is a momentum/semis pullback (SPMO mom5 −5.15, VGT −3.04) and QQQ is slipping FURTHER below its 50-day (−1.57% vs −0.54% pre-market 7/15, mom5 −2.26) — hold the structure, but this is a widening test, so do NOT add index beta here.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 20>50>200; +1.12% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.38% over the 200-day (~693.0), −0.86% off the 52-week high (~757.6). QQQ 706.95 bull-stacked but −1.57% BELOW its 50-day (~718.2). SPMO +14.18% / VGT +14.64% over their 200-days.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds its rising 50-day (~743); add index beta only on a QQQ RECLAIM of its 50-day (~718.2) with mom5 turning positive — NOT while it sits −1.57% below with a negative 5-day.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~743), QQQ losing the 20>50>200 stack, or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: Add XLE vs trim rolling XLI
actions.rationale: The tool-aligned 2-week RRG keeps energy the cleanest rotation INTO: XLE Improving ↑ with the board-best acceleration (accel +2.27, rsMom 102.5, +11.52% over its 200-day) one cross from Leading, while XLI is Lagging and rolling (accel −1.58). The sector model's own read this run: 'Rotate toward XLE as XLV weakens.' Defensives (XLP Leading accel +1.27, XLRE +1.01) are bidding alongside — a rotation away from extended cyclical/tech leaders.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving ↑, accel +2.27, rsMom 102.5, rsRatio 99.79 (one cross from Leading), +11.52% over its 200-day; XLI Lagging, accel −1.58, rsRatio 99.97.
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its rsRatio crossing 100 into Leading; fund by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO Mixed, score 39.4, mom21 −1.98) breaks lower, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: Stretched, rolling semis (SOXX) into relief
actions.rationale: SOXX still has the strongest medium-term structure on the board (+35.83% over its 200-day) but is now Lagging on the 2-week accel (rsMom 98.56, accel −0.95) and rolling hard this morning — rsMom1m −15.62, members MRVL mom5 −22.76 / MU −14.19 / QCOM −10.43 / LRCX −9.92, only 3/12 above their 50-day and 0/12 with up-momentum. Trim the extension into any relief bounce, keep the AI-hardware core — de-risking a stretched, rolling leader, not selling the theme.
actions.structuralAnchor: SOXX 528.82, +35.83% over its 200-day, Lagging (rsMom 98.56 <100, accel −0.95), breadth 8/12 bull-stacked but only 3/12 above the 50-day and 0/12 up-momentum.
actions.trigger: Trim into any relief bounce / on a loss of the rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth (above-50 count) improving.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: MSFT — tentative 50-day reclaim, hold don't chase into 7/29
actions.rationale: The lone structural crack is tentatively repairing: MSFT 404.6 ticked back above its 50-day (~401.7, +0.72%, mom5 +5.27, mom21 +1.21) for the first time in the theme — but it is STILL bear-stacked and −7.72% below its 200-day (~438.4), −24.89% off its high (~538.7). Per anti-whipsaw, HOLD and let the 7/29 capex guide confirm; do NOT call the bottom on one 50-day tick.
actions.structuralAnchor: MSFT 404.6, +0.72% over its 50-day (~401.7) — first reclaim of the theme — but bear-stacked, −7.72% below the 200-day (~438.4) and −24.89% off its 52-week high (~538.7).
actions.trigger: Confirmation = a daily close HOLDING above the 50-day (~401.7) then working toward the 200-day (~438.4); the 7/29 FY26-Q4 print + capex guide is the catalyst.
actions.invalidation: MSFT loses back its 50-day (~402) — the reclaim fails and the structural crack persists into the 7/29 print.

actions.subject: Carry a defined-risk hedge through 7/17 OPEX and the 7/29 tail
actions.rationale: VIX 16.49 is low but FIRMING (from 15.67 at 7/15 AH) as the leaders wobble, and SPY sits only −0.86% off its 52-week high, so carry a small defined-risk put-spread / VIX-call stub through tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX and toward the underpriced 7/29 hawkish-hold tail. Reassess after OPEX rather than paying for a standing short; the exact FedWatch path is STALE/UNVERIFIED and must be re-pulled before rolling toward 7/29.
actions.structuralAnchor: VIX 16.49 (low but rising +0.82 off the 7/15 AH read) with SPY −0.86% off the 52-week high — cheap-but-firming vol into 7/17 OPEX and a live two-way 7/29 policy risk create asymmetric near-term event exposure.
actions.trigger: Carry a small defined-risk stub through 7/17 OPEX; reduce it if the tape stabilizes and QQQ reclaims its 50-day (~718.2), roll toward 7/29 only after re-pulling the live FedWatch path and gamma walls.
actions.invalidation: A benign OPEX + QQQ 50-day reclaim narrows near-term risk (let the stub decay), or VIX pushes >19-20 and the stub is already working.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, 7/16 morning intraday off the 7/15 close: a healthy tech-led bull near all-time highs whose ONE near-term crack is only tentatively repairing while today's tape rotates defensive. SPY 751.11 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.86% off its 52-week high (~757.6), +8.38% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +8.84%/+22.07% — 5th distinct daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's forming 7/16 intraday. The cool June CPI + Q2 bank beats kept the bull intact, but QQQ 706.95 is STILL below its 50-day and slipping further (−1.57% vs −0.54% pre-market 7/15, mom5 −2.26) — the reclaim is moving away, not toward. VIX 16.49 (low but firming +0.82 off 7/15 AH). Three features: (1) leadership rotating DEFENSIVE — XLE the best accel (+2.27) one cross from Leading, XLF back to Leading, XLP/XLRE Leading and XLU Improving on a defensive bid, while XLI (accel −1.58) and XLV roll and stretched semis (SOXX +35.83% >200d, rsMom1m −15.62) sell hard; (2) the momentum complex is today's weak tape (SPMO mom5 −5.15, VGT −3.04) — extended leaders giving back; (3) the lone STRUCTURAL crack MSFT (404.6, bear-stack, −24.89% off its high) TENTATIVELY reclaimed its 50-day (+0.72%, mom5 +5.27) for the first time but is still below its 200-day — watch the 7/29 capex guide before calling a turn. NO clean haven bid — GLD (score 9.8) / SLV (0) firmly Downtrend, BTC-USD Mixed (44.4) but IBIT still Downtrend (24.2); only agriculture (DBA 53.4) is a firm uptrend. Posture: HOLD the bull-stack; ROTATE XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched, rolling semis into relief; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT's tentative reclaim; carry a small defined-risk hedge through 7/17 OPEX; stay OUT of havens.

backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings. The carried estimate for 7/29 was ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% hike, but that split is STALE AND UNVERIFIED this run — treat 7/29 as a live two-way policy risk and re-pull FedWatch before sizing. June CPI printed cool (7/14, web-verified) and June PPI benign (7/15, tape-inferred/unverified); today's 8:30 June retail sales + weekly claims are the next demand-side read, past the release by the 11:00 window but UNVERIFIED. WTI is carried/stale; USO is Mixed (score 39.4, mom21 −1.98).
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia/global-led but on a local-currency, not weak-dollar, basis: EWS (Singapore) leads at 76.8/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +11.1, Strong uptrend), then EWC (Canada) 62.5, EWT (Taiwan) 62 (rel63 +18.16, rel126 +42.76 — the onshore semis/AI-hardware read), EWU (UK) 61.7, EWA 52.4, EWJ 47.9. Nearly every leader carries an FX headwind / confirms-weakness (EWS fxStrength −1.34, EWT −2.47, EWC −2.52; only EWU near-neutral −0.11), so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT's structural breakdown (bear-stack, −24.89% off its high, only tentatively above its 50-day) vs the AI-hardware/semis structure (VGT/SPMO bull-stacked, SOXX +35.83% over its 200-day even after this week's roll) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO clean haven bid: GLD firmly Downtrend (score 9.8, mom63 −16.95, even weaker than the prior run's 14.9), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −29.78); BTC-USD Mixed (44.4, mom21 +7.43) but IBIT still Downtrend (24.2). Agriculture (DBA 53.4, Uptrend) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 44.2) and oil (USO 39.4) are Mixed. Net: risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin — leadership is equities, not havens (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: Cool CPI and orderly bank beats were rewarded, but the QQQ 50-day was NOT won back and this morning the extended leaders (momentum, semis) are giving back while defensives and the beaten-down MSFT bid — a rotation, not a break. What remains uncertain is whether today's retail sales/claims confirm the consumer, whether MSFT's tentative 50-day reclaim holds into its 7/29 capex guide, and whether the 7/17 OPEX pin drags the tape. The exact Fed path is not verified this run and the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail stays underpriced.
backdrop.asymmetry: Downside remains more convex than upside over the next two weeks: SPY is only −0.86% below its 52-week high while VIX is 16.49 and firming, the momentum/semis leaders are rolling, and 7/17 OPEX plus the underpriced 7/29 hike tail sit ahead. That cheap-but-firming vol near the highs supports a small defined-risk hedge through OPEX and, after re-verification, into the Fed; it does not justify adding a standing short.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A QQQ reclaim of its 50-day (~718.2) with positive 5-day (bullish repair), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~742.8, bearish), VIX pushing >19-20, term-structure inversion, a hot retail-sales/claims surprise today, MSFT losing back its 50-day (~402, the repair fails) or reclaiming its 200-day (~438.4, full repair), or the 7/17 OPEX pin dragging the tape. The 7/29 FOMC is the next major policy regime-changer, but its current probabilities require a live re-pull.

attention.title: The 7/29 hawkish-hold hike tail is the standout forward binary — and it is unverified
attention.what: With CPI (7/14) cool and PPI (7/15) tape-inferred benign, the near-term inflation binaries are past. The dominant remaining two-week asymmetry is the 7/29 FOMC: a carried ~46.5% hike tail against a hawkish hold, pairing policy risk with the MSFT capex guide the same afternoon. That split is CARRIED from a prior run and is not verified this run.
attention.why: It is the reason to keep the extended semis/growth trims on and carry defined risk rather than fresh length into month-end. Re-pull CME FedWatch before asserting the split; do not anchor on the stale ~53.5/46.5 estimate. A calm VIX (16.49) means the tail is cheap to hedge.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: SPY 751.11, 20>50>200 bull-stack, 50-day ~742.8 (spot +1.12%), 200-day ~693 (+8.38%), −0.86% off the ~757.6 ATH. SPMO +14.18% over its 200-day. QQQ bull-stacked but −1.57% BELOW its ~718 50-day (mom5 −2.26).
recommendations.trigger: Hold while SPY holds its rising ~742.8 50-day. Add index/growth beta ONLY on a QQQ reclaim of ~718 with mom5 turning positive — not while it sits below with a negative 5-day.
recommendations.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY ~742.8 50-day, SPY losing the 20>50>200 stack, or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.
recommendations.rationale: SPY structure is intact near the highs, but QQQ has now FAILED its 50-day reclaim (mom5 −2.26). Hold the broad core; gate fresh growth beta on the QQQ reclaim rather than adding into a test-not-reclaim.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving ↑, accel +2.27 (best on the board, up 3 straight market-bar dates), rsMom 102.5, rsRatio 99.79 (one cross from Leading); XLI Lagging, accel −1.58 (out 3 straight dates).
recommendations.trigger: Add XLE on its rsRatio crossing 100 into the Leading quadrant; trim XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
recommendations.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative / rsMom drops under 100, or oil breaks lower (real-assets-lab has oil soft) aborting the energy turn.
recommendations.rationale: XLE is the best-accelerating sector and has led the board for three straight market-bar dates; XLI is rolling out hardest. The confirmed cyclical hand-off, one cross from Leading — the sector model's own read.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: XLF Leading, rsRatio 101.65 (highest on the board), rsMom 100.61, accel decelerating +0.90→+0.12; bull-stack (+8.13% over its 200-day).
recommendations.trigger: Re-add only if XLF re-accelerates (rsMom crossing back up) above its rising 50-day; else hold the existing bank-beat position.
recommendations.invalidation: XLF drops out of the top RS band (rsRatio <100) or fails its rising 50-day — the leadership fades and the hold becomes a trim.
recommendations.rationale: The Q2 beats left XLF the highest-RS sector and still Leading, but its 2-week accel has faded to +0.12. Per anti-whipsaw, hold — do not chase a decelerating leader.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: SOXX 528.82, +35.83% over its 200-day, Lagging (rsMom 98.56 <100, accel −0.95), 1-month RS −15.62 (sharpest on the board), bull-stack.
recommendations.trigger: Trim into strength / on SOXX losing its rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth improving.
recommendations.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.
recommendations.rationale: Strongest structure but now Lagging with the sharpest 1-month RS drop and the most extended reading on the board — trim the extension into the 7/29 catalyst, keep the core.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: MSFT 404.60 reclaimed its ~401.7 50-day (ma50Dist +0.72, mom5 +5.27) but is still bear-stack, −7.72% under its ~438 200-day, −24.89% off its high.
recommendations.trigger: Small starter only while MSFT HOLDS above the ~401.7 50-day (first higher-low into the 7/29 print); scale on a confirmed close above the ~438 200-day.
recommendations.invalidation: A close back below the ~401.7 50-day reverts MSFT to the lone-crack read — stand down.
recommendations.rationale: The beaten-down software laggard is reclaiming its 50-day with a strong 5-day pop as semis cool — the AI-capex dispersion flip. Trade the level, not the story; the 7/29 FY26 Q4 capex guide is the binary. Tactical, so capped confidence.

recommendations.structuralAnchor: GLD score 9.8 (down from 14.9) firmly Downtrend; SLV 0; BTC-USD 44.4 Mixed (mom21 +7.43, mom63 −20.84); DBA 53.4 the only firm uptrend — a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin real-asset tape, not a fear tape.
recommendations.trigger: Stay OUT of metals; re-engage only if GLD reclaims an uptrend AND SLV lifts off a 0 score; BTC only on IBIT confirming above its downtrend.
recommendations.invalidation: A Hormuz-driven energy re-spike revives a gold/inflation-hedge bid (GLD reclaims trend) — then the no-haven read is stale and gold becomes the tell into 7/29.
recommendations.rationale: No clean haven bid, and gold is weaker than the prior run — leadership is equities rotating internally, not havens.

events.event: June retail sales + weekly initial jobless claims
events.consensus: RELEASED 8:30 ET this morning (Census retail sales + DOL claims); by the 11:00 ET morning window this is PAST the release but the actual figures are UNVERIFIED this run — TAPE-INFERRED only. Retail control group feeds the Q2 GDP nowcast; XLY/XLP read-through. Re-pull the prints before asserting numbers.

scenarios.name: soft-landing-confirmed
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: constructive — steady spend + contained claims keeps the soft landing intact and supports the confirmed XLF/XLE cyclical rotation; consistent with the calm VIX 16.49 tape

scenarios.name: weak
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: mixed — soft retail / rising claims stokes growth worry but eases the carried 7/29 hike tail

scenarios.name: hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish-at-margin — strong spend + low claims re-arms the ~46.5% hike tail into tomorrow's OPEX

events.psychologyNote: The growth-vs-inflation cross the day before OPEX. Morning price action (growth cooling, XLF/XLE and defensives firming, VIX 16.49) is CONSISTENT with a benign-to-soft read, but this is tape-inferred — probabilities are estimates and the actual figures must be re-pulled before they are asserted as the confirm/deny of the soft landing the cyclical rotation is priced for.

events.consensus: ESTIMATE — standard monthly expiry TOMORROW. With CPI cool and PPI tape-inferred benign, positive dealer gamma should dampen the range unless retail sales/claims surprised. NOTE: Tier-A has NO gamma-wall levels or option term structure this run (gamma/options-structure tool reads not present) — the dealer-gamma regime is UNVERIFIED; re-pull the chain live before assigning walls.

scenarios.name: pin
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: neutral — a positive-gamma pin dampens the range into expiry as the resolved-benign data week settles near the ~757.6 SPY ATH

scenarios.name: unwind-up
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — a continued relief bid is amplified as hedges roll off toward the ATH

scenarios.name: unwind-down
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish — a hot retail-sales surprise flips positive gamma and dealers sell into weakness

events.psychologyNote: The gamma release valve on the data week. With the inflation binaries resolved benign and VIX at 16.49 a pin is plausible, but without live wall levels this run the dealer-gamma regime is an estimate — re-pull the chain before leaning on a pin.

events.event: FOMC decision + MSFT FY26 Q4 print
events.consensus: ESTIMATE — 2:00 ET FOMC decision (non-SEP, no new dot plot); carried FedWatch split ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% hike is STALE AND UNVERIFIED this run. MSFT reports AMC the same day (capex guide is the AI-software tell). Re-verify both the policy path (fresh FedWatch) and the earnings timing before the event.

scenarios.name: hawkish-hold
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: neutral-to-bearish — hold with a hawkish statement keeps the wide hike tail live, no cut relief, pressure on the most-extended semis/growth

scenarios.name: hike
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish — the carried hike tail delivers, a genuine regime shock into a tape sitting near the highs

scenarios.name: dovish-hold
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — hold with softened language re-opens a cut path, long-duration bid

events.psychologyNote: The dominant month-end binary pairs policy risk with the MSFT capex guide — and MSFT is separately reclaiming its 50-day into it. The policy split is CARRIED and unverified this run, so use the retail-sales read and a fresh FedWatch pull to update the tree rather than anchoring on the stale estimate.

events.consensus: ESTIMATE (re-verify at release) — 8:30 ET payrolls + unemployment + AHE. JUST BEYOND the ~10-trading-day window; carried here as the labor-side confirm/deny to the 7/29 FOMC path.

scenarios.name: goldilocks
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bullish — moderate payrolls + steady unemployment supports the soft landing without re-arming the hawkish path

scenarios.name: hot
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: bearish-at-margin — strong payrolls + wage heat compounds any post-FOMC hawkish tail

scenarios.name: weak
scenarios.expectedEffect: overall: mixed — soft payrolls stokes growth worry but eases the rate path

events.psychologyNote: The next month's labor anchor — outside the near-term window but the follow-through to whether the post-7/29 regime reads hawkish or soft-landing. Probabilities are estimates to be re-pulled closer to the print.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, tech-led bull that RESOLVED its densest binaries of the month benignly — cool June CPI, broad bank beats, a benign (tape-inferred) PPI — but is now DIGESTING near the highs, with the extended leaders giving back and the crowd rotating defensive. VIX 16.49 (low but firming +0.82 off the 7/15 after-hours read), SPY bull-stacked yet −0.86% off the ATH, and QQQ 706.95 STILL below its 50-day and slipping (−1.57%, mom5 −2.26) rather than reclaiming. The crowd's posture is RELIEVED-BUT-ROTATING — the fat left tail (hot CPI / bank miss) is gone, so profits are being taken in the stretched momentum/semis complex (SPMO mom5 −5.15, VGT −3.04, SOXX rsMom1m −15.62, 0/12 up-momentum) and recycled into energy (XLE accel +2.27) and defensives (XLP/XLRE/XLU bidding), while the beaten-down MSFT gets a tentative bid (first 50-day reclaim, +0.72%). NOTE the deterministic composite score of 0 is VIX-only (Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run), NOT a measured neutral and NOT a data gap — VIX is present, low and firming (16.49). The subtle danger is COMPLACENCY dressed as rotation: the crowd treats cool CPI as an all-clear on the Fed, but the 7/29 hike tail (carried ~46.5%, STALE/UNVERIFIED) is a live two-way risk into 7/17 OPEX and today's demand data. That is the underpriced near-term asymmetry. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push back >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~742.8) (bull failure), a hot retail-sales/claims print today, the market pricing/delivering the ~46.5% 7/29 hike (the biggest single falsifier), or — bullishly — QQQ reclaiming its 50-day (~718.2) with positive 5-day and MSFT holding above its 50-day and reclaiming ~438 (a broadening that ends the lone crack).
inputs: SPY 751.11 bull-stack (20>50>200), +1.12% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.38% over the 200-day (~693.0), −0.86% off the 52-week high (~757.6); mom126/252 +8.84% / +22.07% (Tier-A morning read, 7/16 18:19Z, LIVE intraday off the 7/15 close)

5th DISTINCT daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's forming 7/16 intraday — persistence by distinct market-bar dates, not intraday reruns. QQQ 706.95 is STILL below its 50-day and slipping (−1.57% vs −0.54% pre-market 7/15, mom5 −2.26) — reclaim moving AWAY

June CPI WEB-VERIFIED cool (headline −0.4% MoM / core 0.0% MoM); Q2 big banks WEB-VERIFIED beat — both carried from prior web research; June PPI (7/15) benign but TAPE-INFERRED / UNVERIFIED; today's 8:30 retail sales + claims past the release but UNVERIFIED this run

RRG rotates DEFENSIVE: XLE Improving ↑/into (accel +2.27, board best, one cross from Leading); XLF back to Leading (rsRatio 101.65, highest); XLP Leading (accel +1.27), XLRE Leading (+1.01), XLU Improving; XLI Lagging (accel −1.58) and XLV Weakening roll; SOXX Lagging (accel −0.95, rsMom1m −15.62); MAGS Leading (accel +0.33) on thin breadth (1/7 bull-stacked)
VIX 16.49 (low but firming +0.82 off 7/15 AH 15.67); Fear&Greed NOT fetched → composite is VIX-only (score 0 = VIX-only, NOT a data gap). Term structure not in Tier-A (re-pull live)

MSFT 404.6 ticked +0.72% back above its 50-day (~401.7, mom5 +5.27) — first reclaim of the theme — but STILL bear-stacked and −7.72% below its 200-day (~438.4); the lone structural crack tentatively repairing into the 7/29 capex guide
Real assets: no clean haven bid — GLD 9.8 / SLV 0 firmly Downtrend; BTC-USD 44.4 Mixed but IBIT 24.2 Downtrend; DBA 53.4 the only firm uptrend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Forward catalysts: TODAY 7/16 8:30 retail sales + claims (past release, unverified), 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC + MSFT AMC — the carried ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail is STALE/UNVERIFIED and must be re-pulled
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case is a bullish slow-grind that is now DIGESTING: the leadership index has NOT reclaimed its 50-day and the extended momentum/semis leaders are rolling, so the cleaner edge is HOLDING and PRESSING the confirmed rotation (rotate XLI→XLE, trim stretched semis into relief) — not adding index beta at the highs while QQQ slips below its 50-day, and not chasing MSFT's one-tick 50-day reclaim before the 7/29 guide. The one probability tilt AGAINST the crowd: the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail (STALE/UNVERIFIED) is underpriced by a relief tape treating cool CPI as an all-clear — carry that as the near-term asymmetry through 7/17 OPEX (watch energy / VIX term structure as the tell), not a standing short. Re-fetch VIX term structure and the live gamma walls before sizing anything off the vol/positioning read.

groups.label: Magnificent 7

notable.reason: group leader sitting AT its 52-week high (0% off) and the only bull-stacked member (mom21 +11.39, +20.74% over its 200-day) — the one clean structure carrying the complex
biggest 21d surge on the board (mom21 +12.86 / mom5 +6.07) but still bear-stacked (−14.93% off its high) — momentum without the structure yet
the structural laggard just ticking back above its 50-day (mom5 +5.27, +0.72% over it) yet still bear-stacked −7.72% below the 200-day (−24.89% off high) — a crack starting to mend, not mended
bear-stacked laggard (−20.44% off high, −6.61% below its 200-day); mom5 −4.13 the only red 5-day in the complex, not confirming the turn

groups.note: MAGS is Leading/into on a modest 2-week accel (+0.33, rsMom 100.68 >100) — the mega-cap complex firming, though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked (breadth 1/7; above50 4/7, above200 5/7). Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL sits AT its 52-week high (the only bull-stack, +20.74% over its 200-day) and META's +12.86 mom21 / +6.07 mom5 surge carries the tape, while NVDA/AMZN/GOOGL are firm-but-tangled just under their 50-days. The 5-day is green across six of seven (only TSLA −4.13 red). The tell to WATCH is MSFT: it just ticked back above its 50-day (mom5 +5.27, +0.72%) but stays bear-stacked −7.72% below the 200-day (~438) — a mending crack, not a repair. Confirm on breadth broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) and MSFT holding the 50-day + reclaiming the 200-day. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

groups.label: Semiconductors

notable.reason: sharpest reversal on the board (mom5 −22.76 / mom21 −39.16, −40.62% off its high) — the profit-taking tell, now −20.09% below its 50-day
most extended member (mom63 +86.54, +77.47% over its 200-day, mom252 +609.6) — the extension risk into any air-pocket, mom5 −14.19
strongest structural trend (mom63 +92.32, +68.28% over its 200-day), still bull-stacked right at its 50-day (+0.05%) but mom5 −9.2 fading with the group
the AI-hardware bellwether that just lost its 50-day (−4.66%) with mom5 −7.27 — the internal-thinning tell under the strong ETF structure

groups.note: SOXX is Lagging/neutral, quad A (rsMom 98.56 <100, 2-week accel −0.95) yet still the board's strongest STRUCTURE — 8/12 bull-stacked, 12/12 above the 200-day, +35.83% over it. But the internals are collapsing short-term: upMom is 0/12 (not one member with positive short momentum), above-50-day breadth fell to 3/12, and the 5-day is red across the whole complex except NVDA (+2.04). MRVL (mom5 −22.76 / mom21 −39.16, −40.62% off its high) is the profit-taking tell; MU/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/AMD sit far above trend = extension risk; and bellwether TSM just lost its 50-day (−4.66%). rsMom1m −15.62 confirms the 1-month RS bleed. TRIM the extension into any PPI-relief pop / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 with above-50 breadth improving — de-risking a stretched leader with fading internals, not selling the AI-hardware theme. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

experimental.title: Cross-tool risk-on confirmation: haven-abstinence vs defensive-out / cyclical-in rotation
experimental.note: Method: on each fresh bar, cross the real-assets haven leg against the sector RRG rotation vector — flag a 'clean risk-on' state when (a) both monetary havens are in downtrends AND (b) the defensive sector is rotating out while a cyclical rotates in, with no clean Leading defensive. Event-study forward bench drift conditioned on the joint state vs either signal alone. THIS RUN it fires: real-assets shows GLD 9.8 and SLV 0 both Downtrend (mom63 −16.95 / −29.78) — no haven bid — while sector RRG has XLV (defensive) Weakening ↓ rotating out (accel −1.11) and XLE (cyclical) Improving ↑ rotating in (accel +2.27), and XLK the nominal leader only Lagging. The two owning tools each see half; the joint 'havens dead AND defensives rolling out' state is the cross-tool confirmation no single tool captures. Inputs: real-assets GLD/SLV/BTC trend+mom63, sector per-name rrgState/rotation/accel, bench MA-stack. One observation logged; needs a persisted prior-bar snapshot to compute the state-transition delta.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Improving/Leading→Weakening/Peaking) as a state-transition timing layer
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector crossing out of a strong quad (Leading→Peaking / Improving→Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar, then event-study forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the next scheduled catalyst confirms or denies the roll. THIS RUN the detector flags the XLV→XLE hand-off: XLV Weakening ↓ (quad W, accel −1.11, rsMom6m −4.71) rolling out while XLE Improves ↑ (quad I, accel +2.27, rsMom 102.5) rotates in, with leader XLK only Lagging (accel −0.57) and no clean Leading GICS sector — a defensive-to-cyclical hand-off, not yet a persistent leadership change (MA stack under XLE still tangled), so it is actionable as a tilt but flagged for persistence per anti-whipsaw. The value is the state-transition TIMING, which no owning tool persists today. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history across bars + each sector's next dated catalyst + realized outcome.
